steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize a similarity between the two specimens. That means the answer must compare Mara and Elias directly, highlighting something they share — not describe just one of them or contrast them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The dinosaur fossil specimens Mara and Elias are both members of the genus *Tyrannosaurus*.": Directly states what the two specimens have in common. Emphasizes the shared genus. ✓
  • "While Mara is housed at the Field Museum of Natural History, Elias is housed at the Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County.": Uses "while" to contrast their locations — emphasizes a difference, not a similarity. ✗
  • "The Field Museum of Natural History, where Mara is housed, is located in Chicago, Illinois.": Only describes Mara's museum. No comparison with Elias at all. ✗
  • "Elias is the nickname of a *Tyrannosaurus* fossil specimen housed at the Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County in Los Angeles, California.": Only describes Elias. No comparison with Mara. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
